desc:
A large bed dominates the room. Two paintings of Mirkwood hang on the western
wall. A window showing the forested valley of Rivendell is in the northern wall,
and against the southern wall stands a large closet.
name: Naril's Chambers

desc:
This room takes its name from a great hearth at the north end, in which a bright
fire burns continuously. On either side, carven pillars extend along the length
of the room. The cosy atmosphere makes this a favourite meeting place for guests
of Elrond.
name: Hall of Fire

desc:
Large bookshelves completly cover all walls, interrupted only by two small
doorways, one leading east and the other west. Four large armchairs made of
rugged leather stand around a low oaken table. As there are no windows, the
light is provided by lanterns positioned in several locations around the
library. The floor is covered by large carpets woven of wool.
name: Naril's Library

desc:
You are now in the hallway to and old building, on the walls there are some
picture of old people. The floor is tatterd from many years of hard use. On the
floor an carpet is put, it has stripes in different colour.
name: Hallway

desc:
This is a small library, that the owner has been collection books for in many
years. The walls are coverd with bookshelf that contain many book, most of them
are about the powers of magic. An small sofa is standing in the corner of the
room, and it is ragged from many years of use.
name: Library

desc:
In the right corner of this simple room there is a small fireplace emitting a
warm glow. For some reason the dense smoke found in the adjacent room does not
enter this room, perhaps because of the warm fire. A small table stands in the
left corner and next to it there is a bed with white sheets. On the wall four
hooks can be seen hanging while a red carpet lies across the floor looking
rather clean and in good shape.
name: Olorion's Bedroom

desc:
Located off to the side of the Hall of Fire, this residence is simply but
comfortably furnished in typical elven fashion. Placed flush with the northern
wall is a single bed, next to which is a desk that is nearly buried in various
maps of Arda. Along the western wall, multiple bookshelves stand, containing all
types of books and writing equipment. Against the southern wall a lone cushioned
chair sits, worn down over the years until almost threadbare.
name: Aiddar's Bedchamber

desc:
Most noticeable in this small suite are the intricate carvings upon the marble
walls. Upon close inspection they seem to tell the history of the Noldor. Tales
of the Kinslaying, the Helcaraxe, and the story of the Silmarils each have their
own place of honour. Glory, defeat, joy, tears - all are depicted here.
name: Ankor's Bower

desc:
This is a beautiful short hallway that allows access to the suites where Elrond
and Arwen live. A faint sweet aroma tinges the air from the fragrant woods that
panel the walls. On the east wall is a large window that is often left open,
weather permitting. Ornately carved oak doors lie to the north and south.
name: Private Hallway

desc:
You stand in a beautifully decorated room, with arched windows on every wall and
many comfortable chairs. A rainbow of colourful flowers grow from ornate
planters, filling the air with a fresh, pleasant scent. Glancing around, you do
not notice any valuable jewellery or money.
name: Arwen's Suite

desc:
The room is tastefully decorated with long curtains and art objects of elvish
make, which are much too heavy to carry out of the room. You see much fine
furniture including a huge desk with many drawers. A lovely fireplace,
generously piled with firewood, is built into the south wall.
name: Elrond's Suite

desc:
High above the ground, a graceful archway leads to the northern part of the Last
Homely Home. The sides of the archway are decorated with white, fine carvings of
flowers and leaves that intermingle with real leaves and climbing plants. The
arch makes a slow curve towards the east and south. On the eastern side is the
Fountain square to be seen while the view opens to the west and north over a
blooming, prospering valley.
name: Graceful Archway

desc:
The white, wooden platform measures at least seven or eight yards in diameter
and its borders are decorated and marked with wide flowerboxes. Many colourful
and well-grown plants thrive in the boxes and climb on filigrane supports made
of light brown wood. Above is a roof made of darker wood. It is also overgrown
and under it hang flowerpots from which plants seem to flow like water. A wind
chime hangs from the southern border of the roof, so that each small gust of
wind produces silent chimes.
name: White Platform with a Pavilion

desc:
The fine carvings on this roofed archway's banister are barely visible, as the
entire archway is heavily overgrown with healthy, green and blooming plants. The
plants grow up from below, from hanging flowerpots as well as from well-tended
flowerboxes made of light clay standing on the ground. Towards the north, the
archway becomes wide enough to support a couple of Rivendell's small, filigrane
housings; all of them are also heavily decorated and sometimes overgrown with
dense, healthy vegetation of all kinds.
name: An Overgrown Archway

desc:
The archway becomes much wider as it runs through a long, oval shaped garden.
Between white statues showing elves and scenes from the ancient times stand
other finely crafted shapes that represent growing plants and trees. All the
statues and climb aids are overgrown by countless different flowers or other
plants; between those plants stand white benches. Northwards, there are a few
small housings to be seen which have been elegantly fitted within the thriving
vegetation of this high garden. To the east, the archway transforms into narrow
stairs which lead steeply upwards. The ground below, as a look over the flowered
banister reveals, is at least twenty yards below.
name: The High Garden

desc:
This part of the Last Homely Home consists of a small number of strewn, separate
housings that are connected by raised walkways. Every so often, one of the
walkways connects to another one, thus creating some sort of seemingly
weightless net, as the slender supports are well-hidden in the vegetation
growing from below.
name: The Northern Last Homely Home

desc:
The building becomes very wide as it opens up to a large open area where several
gardens, separated by massive hedges, have been created. Strewn all over the
garden are small pavilions, gazebos, statues, benches, and occasional ponds in
which shimmering fishes of all kinds and sizes swim. There even seem to be a few
smaller housings that have inspiciously been crafted - or grown? - within these
prospering gardens. Right in the centre of this area splashes a large fountain.
name: In the Pavilion Gardens

desc:
The building has been skilfully built into the landscape, making it hard to tell
where the vegetation starts and the building ends. It winds itself over and
around the terrain and seems to have grown right out of the bushes, vines and
trees that surround it. Once in a while pavilions, statues, benches, or small
niches invite for a rest or to take a look at the numerous flourishing plants
and the ever-blooming flowers around. A steep stair rises towards the south and
actually leads more upwards than southwards towards a curving bridge.
name: Winding Building

desc:
The Last Homely Home here is made from stone, but the brown material could very
well be hard wood. The building's decorations mimic growing vines or even
thicker trees that intermingle with actual ever-green vegetation. The stairs
here lead down towards a path and spiral upwards around a wide yet still
graceful tower. Every so often, the stair widens into a larger balcony.
name: Northern Entrance of the Last Homely Home

desc:
This large and mostly untended garden features countless wild flowers, long
grasses, and occasional bushes. Whenever a plant seems to reach the end of
flouriscence other flowers take over, so the garden is always filled with an
ocean of colourful dots of petals. Many narrow paths snake through the high
grasses and around the thickest patches of flowers, and several bee hives can be
seen all over the landscape. Countless bees are constantly buzzing around,
flying from flower to flower and come and go from the hive. Here and there, one
can spot gracefully built and with higher vegetation entwined houses of
Rivendell.
name: The Bees' Garden

desc:
A narrow path curves itself around larger bushes, groups of trees and statues
made of white marble showing ancient warriors. It raises a bit towards the north
and finally leads to one of the entrances of the sprawled Last Homely Home. The
northern part seems to consist of small pavillons, archways, and small housings
decorated with countless small features. Many of the walls have been climbed by
blooming plants such as clematis, roses, and white-blooming ivy.
name: Path towards the Northern Part of Elrond's Home

desc:
This high bridge is very narrow and seems fragile although neither your feet nor
the winds seem to be able to shake it in the slightest way. The banisters have
been carefully carved over years - or even decades - to show times and events of
old. On the banisters stand narrow flowerboxes of clay in which countless
well-smelling herbs of healing or appeal grow.
name: A High, White Bridge

desc:
The banisters have been replaced in this part of the bridge with dense, green
hedges which grow on both sides of the pathway. The bridge curves several times
to the left and to the right, making it much longer than it would have been if
it ran straight. The bridge descends a bit while curving towards the north until
it finally ends at a small, flowered platform where a relatively steep stair
leads downwards.
name: The Curving Bridge

desc:
A benevolent magical aura embraces all who enter this shadowy demense. It looks
like the light dances and sparkles softly around the room, especially upon a
desk standing in the middle of the room, covered with potions and runic scrolls.
The shadows grow deeper in the corner where a neatly kept pallet is located for
sleeping. Attached to the far wall is an exercise apparatus to increase agility
and speed.
name: Finglorion's Hidden Workshop

desc:
A calm, green light is suffusing from above, enlightening a network of wide,
solid branches which form the floor of this simple but comfortable house located
high above the ground. Looking up one can see leaves, leaves and more leaves,
leaving just enough room in between them to see sun shining or stars glinting.
The dense layer of leaves continues on small branches on every side of the room,
and some of the leaves seem to be covered with strange runes. One of the
underbranches has a natural waterhole, suitable to collect water that has rained
down.
name: Arcanum's Branch

desc:
The study of Oropher the elven Wizard is full of light, and its air is thick and
warm. The room has been furnished both for the studies and relaxation: a large,
wooden desk is littered with documents, scrolls and maps of old, while a
comfortable-looking armchair stands next to a large, decorated window. The scent
of pipeweed hangs in the air, originating from a small, wooden box on the
windowsill, hinting where the wizard likes to sit and ponder the mysteries of
the world. The window faces to the west, offering a magnificent view to the
valley of Imladris. Among the trees and plants outside many birds and smaller
animals can be heard; the tranquility of Rivendell is very profound.
name: Oropher's Study

desc:
An incredibly large bed takes centre-stage in this room, fit for a family of
giants it is covered with silk sashes and pillows in all sizes, furs made for
covers are lining the bottom and a huge purple drape comes from the ceiling
ready to hide the bed completely. A large vanity is set against the top, boxes
spilling over jewelry, an incense burner on the side. See-through silk leggings
are draped over the chair in front, a huge chest filled with boots of all sizes
lies in the corner, and suggestive artwork covers the walls.
name: Zagabah's Parlour

desc:
This wooden, light-coloured room contains a big bed and a large window which
allows the sun to shine in during the day time and offers a view towards the
forest on the northern slopes of the Rivendell Valley. There are pictures and
paintings on the walls and one of them illustrating a large red dove stands out
from the rest. The floor is covered with a thick red mat. Other furniture
consists of a table, a few chairs and a cupboard.
name: Dhufa's Hideout

desc:
This cottage is small and simple in design, consisting of but a single room. The
colours are muted, consisting of various shades of brown: the brown stones of
the walls, the dark-stained wooden planks of the floor, and the light, unstained
undersurface of the peaked wooden roof above. A loft is nestled beneath the
roof, covering perhaps half the area of the floor, with a wooden ladder
providing access. A single table with two chairs sits in the middle of the room,
and aside from the fireplace that dominates the northern wall, is the only
furniture. Large windows are set in the eastern and western walls, with
dark-stained wooden shutters that have been thrown wide.
name: Cottage in the Pavilion Gardens

desc:
This cosy, small structure has been skillfully built as an extention from the
vegetation clinging to the overgrown archways. The simple room is large enough
to fit some essential personal belongings of a traveller, in addition to a table
and some cabinets there is just enough space for a single bed on the
finely-woven vine structure of the floor. There are no windows, but nevertheless
sunlight penetrates through the canvice of vegetation that makes up most of the
walls and ceiling.
name: Enclosed in the Vinework

desc:
This chamber is made out of wood from the black pine tree. The dark colours of
the room are contrasted by a marble altar standing in the middle of the room.
There is a bed in one of the corners, a desk with a chair next to the window,
and a large bookshelf standing against one of the walls. The window shows a
beautiful scenery of Rivendell.
name: Eur's Chamber

desc:
Entering the domicile, the foyer gives way to a cluttered, messy room. The walls
have been overtaken by an assortment of plants and vines. From the ceiling the
dense vegetation hangs low, and is apparently very healthy. In the center of the
room rests a giant boulder covered in strange markings. A diverse collection of
pipes lies on the table in the corner next to an antique clock, while bits of
trash and pipe-weed litter the floor. The inhabitants of this dwelling clearly
lack any form of personal hygiene.
name: Cashel's Entwined Cottage

desc:
This medium-sized room looks like a mixture between a library, a laboratory and
a geographer's workshop. A number of shelves cover the walls, each and every
shelf filled with heavy tomes and ancient-looking books, some of them stored in
glass boxes apparently to preserve them from exposure. Piles of yellow scrolls
and parchments are lying on top of the books. A wooden desk stands in front of
the windows. A great part of the desk is covered with a spread-out map of the
Misty Mountains east of Rivendell. Some quill pens and vials of ink rest on the
map, to force it to stay open, and to be always nearby, available when needed.
Other maps hang from the walls, showing places both near and far.
name: Sarion's Study

desc:
The pleasant warmth of a bright fireplace welcomes all friendly visitors to the
house of an Elven wizard, explorer and part-time orc hunter Sarion. A large
wooden table, surrounded by chairs, stands in the middle of the room, upon a
brightly coloured carpet. In the southwest corner of the room a sofa and a
couple of armchairs offer a more comfortable way to rest. A mantelpiece,
definitely unusual in its nature, stands beside the inner door. Wide windows on
the northern wall offer a staggering view to the Misty Mountains.
name: Sarion's Relaxation Chamber

desc:
In a quiet section of Rivendell stands this humble home. While modest in size,
the grand views of the valley impress beyond imagination. Flowerbeds surround
the windows filling the air with a sweet fragrance. A small bed sits under the
south window which frames the cascading waterfall above. On the west wall a
simple kitchen lined with cupboards catches the eye. The cupboards are filled
with delicate plates and glasses of the highest quality.
name: Elianith's Garden

desc:
This narrow but tall room has been built simultaneously both in ascetic and in
luxurious way. There are only few very simple furniture but the decorations
around the walls and on above dome are marvellous, hinting about the great skill
and lots of time required to create them. Engraved silver plates cover the
walls, and between the plates occasional bright dots of mithril shine like stars
on the night sky. The corners of the room rise up like pillars which bend
towards each other to form a round dome above. A small alcove exists at the
backside of the room, with a simple bed for sleeping.
name: Small Temple for Elbereth

desc:
Walls of ivory enclose the serene chamber here, slightly glistening in the dim
light. There are four candle holders fastened into every corner of the room;
each holding a long, slender candle which emits a delicate light. A bed stands
along the northern wall, its hand-crafted woodwork matching the small dresser
besides it. The threshold to the balcony stands along the eastern wall, draped
with an immense amount of laced ivy.
name: Elsurion's Chamber

desc:
Hundreds of ivy strands are weaved together along the balcony, draped along all
three sides from above. Between the twined strands at a closer glance, one can
notice the glossy, ivory rails that form the patio itself, connecting at its
base to a matching ivory floor. The ivy is thick in most places and it creates a
very secluded and private area to relax within.
name: Ivy-draped Balcony

desc:
In a silent corner of the winding building is a surprisingly spacious abode,
perfect for a small bed. The room is decorated sparsely with a handful of maps,
many of which are faded and hardly recognisable due to heavy usage. A bookshelf,
worn at the edges as if itself had once been read from cover to cover, is the
other singular piece of furniture. A tidy and clean atmosphere sets the
surroundings. A selection of aged, yet clean rugs, slightly mismatched in colour
have been placed carefully across the floor, small areas of pine appearing among
the gaps, creating the impression of wooden slats between the fading colours.
Cutting across the ceiling is a single bolt of lightning, carved into the
granite roof, appearing to lack the deliberate care of the rest of the room.
name: Chamber of Lightning

desc:
The stair that winds upwards on the outside ends on a large terrace in the
north. The view opens over the vineyards in that direction and finally meets the
end of the valley. The floor of the tower is made of soft wood and all around
are large flowerpots from which countless plants and vines grow that merge with
the building's graceful decorations. The round tower is wide enough to feature
two livings, one to the east and one to the west. A small, white stair winds
itself upwards towards a third home.
name: The Tower of the North

desc:
The old wooden floor is strewn by various chemical journals and dusty scrolls.
The meagre and rather tasteless furniture coupled with the thousands of pages of
chemical journals, stacked on top of an old oak desk alongside numerous
laboratory flasks, leads you to believe that the owner is likely not an aspiring
architect but rather an acknowledged wizard. A dusty golden harp hangs on the
wall, possibly once used to play soothing tunes and provide occasional refuge
for an otherwise chemically obsessed mind.
name: Day's Laboratory

desc:
This room is made of grey stones and beautiful, dark-brown wood. It has a
peaceful, yet mysterious athmosphere, created by a magical aura that is faintly
shimmering on all surfaces. There is a painting of a great wizard on one of the
walls - Sylvari himself performing some strange incantations. Bookshelves have
been stacked from the floor to the ceiling with scrolls and old books. Their
strange, arcane look suggests that many of them are enchanted, or even cursed,
making it very unwise to touch anything.
name: Sylvari's House of Meditation

desc:
This room is small and simply furnished, in the fashion of the wood elves. There
is a variety of house plants arranged under the tall windows, most of which do
not grow in the hidden vale. Crowding the windows, the plants give the abode a
wild appearance. The back of the room has a massive tree as a part of the
foundation. The trunk of the tree is lined with deep recesses in its bark, which
are being used as shelves, now filled with books on a variety of magical and
mundane topics. Instead of a bed, a soft silk hammock hangs in one corner, with
a large pillow undearneath it, acting like a bed for a dog.
name: Ravonor's Sylvan Abode

desc:
You enter what appears to have been the home of a great queen bee. The hive has
been hollowed out leaving hexagonal designs only around the edges of the
structure. Light shining through the top of the hive shows Elven decorations and
furnishings about the house. A buzzing noise resonates below.
name: Faehran's Refurbished Beehive

desc:
Once large and strong, the oak is now past its better years. The hollow of the
tree has been used as a secure hive by bees for ages. Here and there are the
remains of honeycombs filling the cracks. Even though bees have abandoned it for
some reason, the scent of old oak is mixed with one of honey. A leather armchair
stands in the room.
name: Old Hive

desc:
This round chamber at the bottom level of the tower is quite empty and ascetic,
giving the feeling that the owner is someone who journeys a lot. The only
furniture in the room are a bed, a table and a chair. There are some writings on
the wall, appearing to list the orcs that this wizard has slain in the battle. A
distant, magical aura lies in the room.
name: Akz's Magical Quarter
